chunks.content:
Article: Can they speak English
[]: Can they speak English?             Quote Post by Ayana-Me » Mon Jul 02, 2007 4:05 pm Does anyone know that if there is any evidence within the show or manga that the main characters (I assume Asuka knows English) can speak English? I believe that the manga shows letters written by Misato in English (perhaps that was a Viz translation?). But other than that there is a good deal of prominently displayed English signs in NERV headquarters (maybe that's cuz it's a U.N. organization and the US has the U.N. by the nuts). I also recall Ritsuko using an English language laptop in EoE. Either way, can we assume that the main characters of Eva are bilingual?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Jul 02, 2007 4:37 pm I doubt there's anything more at work here than the usual Japanese fascination with using English because it looks cool.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Jul 02, 2007 4:50 pm why do they think English is cool? (as opposed to say, German)

[]:             Quote Post by Ayana-Me » Mon Jul 02, 2007 5:05 pm I assume that's because anime originated from Disney cartoons and therefore has an innate American influence. There are many other aspects of American culture that have influenced Japan: hip hop, western clothing, game shows (sadly).

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Jul 02, 2007 5:41 pm After the war, with a large American presence in the country, they were exposed to a lot of English. Written and spoken English is seen as a very modern language, as opposed to written Japanese which would be old fashioned. As it is modern they tend to associate it with more high-tech stuff. For the same reason a gaijin might get a tattoo of a Kanji character because it looks cool, English writing looks modern and cool to them. Or so I'm told...

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Jul 02, 2007 5:42 pm "How lucky you Americans are to find the toilet so amusing. For us, it is a mundane and functional item. For you, it is the basis of an entire culture."

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jul 02, 2007 6:22 pm I'd think both Misato and Kaji know at least SOME German since each lived with Asuka there for awhile at different points (first Misato then Kaji. "Oh. Misato was the person who I lived with before you, Kaji-san."~22' ). Asuka's an American Citizen despite being born in Germany, so yeah, she probably knows English as well as her native German tongue and nihongo . teh Rei: Possibly knows a foreign language or two; don't have anything to back it up besides how in episode 9 upon meeting Asuka, she was apparently reading a German book. Shinji seems like he doesn't know any other language since he couldn't "think in German" at all! ;) Though he at least picked up the basic term "Guten Morgen" or at least figured it out based on context. ...Jeez this post has a lot about Germany in it. ^^; @ V: What's the quote from? And your mom doesn't get the point about Eva! It's meant to show how people can HEAL from Depression! (Though my family probably doesn't "get it" either.)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jul 02, 2007 6:58 pm Misato probably knows English. In #08, she addresses the grouchy old captain of the American fleet, and -- tropes of film aside -- it would make oodles more sense for her to be "doing as the Romans do".

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jul 02, 2007 8:37 pm Japanese breaks down into a syllabery with five basic vowels and a very limited repertoire of consonants. Languages like English and German contain many phonemes and structures that are simply not present in Japanese. (For example, the only terminal consonant available in Japanese is n ; every other one must have a vowel attached.) This is why the pronunciation of foreign words is approximated using the available sounds, and we end up with things like "YU- A- NAMBA- WAN". I imagine that, for a Japanese speaker, English/German/etc. speech would be extremely difficult to master.
